title: Peace Day for a Great Leader
story: Every October 2, India celebrates Gandhi Jayanti to honor Mahatma Gandhi, one of history's greatest peace leaders. People gather at his memorials to pray, sing songs, and remember his teachings about non-violence and kindness. Schools hold special programs where kids dress up like Gandhi and share his famous quotes about treating everyone with respect and solving problems without fighting.
activity_question: How do people typically celebrate Gandhi Jayanti?
activity_choices: By gathering at memorials and singing songs
activity_answer: By gathering at memorials and singing songs
follow_up_question: What peaceful ways can you solve disagreements with friends or family?

title: Peace Power: International Day of Non-Violence
story: Every October 2, people around the world celebrate the International Day of Non-Violence by promoting peace and kindness. Schools organize special assemblies where kids learn about solving problems with words instead of fighting. Communities hold peaceful marches, art contests, and storytelling events that show how powerful gentle actions can be. Many people wear white clothing or peace symbols to show their support for making the world a safer, friendlier place for everyone.
activity_question: What do schools often organize on International Day of Non-Violence?
activity_choices: Special assemblies about solving problems peacefully
activity_answer: Special assemblies about solving problems peacefully
follow_up_question: What peaceful action could you take to make your school or neighborhood kinder?

title: Scallop Up Some Fun!
story: Chefs across America fire up their fryers on October 2 for National Fried Scallops Day! These sweet, tender sea treasures get coated in crispy batter and dunked in bubbling oil until golden brown. Restaurants serve them with tartar sauce, lemon wedges, or spicy mayo for dipping. Food lovers post photos of their favorite scallop dishes online, and coastal towns often feature special deals at seafood shacks and fancy restaurants alike.
activity_question: What do chefs coat scallops with before frying them?
bonus_fact: Scallops can actually swim by clapping their shells together like underwater castanets!

title: Your Car Needs a Name Too!
story: Cars everywhere get special names on October 2 during National Name Your Car Day! People give their vehicles fun nicknames like "Betsy," "Lightning," or "The Beast." Some drivers choose names based on their car's color, like "Blue Thunder" or "Red Rocket." Others pick names that match their car's personality - is it reliable like "Trusty" or speedy like "Zoom"? Many people share their creative car names on social media and even put name stickers on their bumpers to show off their newly-named rides!
activity_question: What are some ways people choose names for their cars on National Name Your Car Day?
activity_choices: Based on the car's color or personality
Only using names that start with the letter Z
By asking their pets to pick the name
Using only numbers instead of words
activity_answer: Based on the car's color or personality
follow_up_question: What would you name your family's car and why?

title: Around the World in 80 Days Challenge
story: Every October 2, adventure lovers celebrate Phileas Fogg Wager Day by honoring the famous fictional character who bet he could travel around the entire world in just 80 days! In Jules Verne's classic story, Fogg starts his incredible journey on this exact date. Today, people mark the day by planning their own dream trips, sharing travel photos, or reading adventure books. Some schools organize geography challenges where kids race to name countries and capitals. Travel bloggers post their craziest vacation stories, and libraries host Around the World reading marathons. It's the perfect day to let your imagination wander to far-off places!
activity_question: According to the story, what did Phileas Fogg bet he could do in 80 days?
bonus_fact: Jules Verne wrote this famous adventure story in 1873, inspiring travelers for over 150 years!
